items.title: 'Epic in scale': APY Lands exhibition opens at NGA after three-year delay

children.content: An exhibition of First Nations art from the APY Lands in South Australia brings together 30 large-scale works by nearly 50 artists.

image.altText: Two woman sitting on while painting.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: 53 witnesses, 97 exhibits: Murder trial jury faces 'complex' task

children.content: Justice David Mossop told the jury it had a "complex" and "substantial" task after the court was presented with 53 witnesses, 14 statements or recordings, and 97 exhibits.

image.altText: A woman smiles with the sun on her face.

items.title: High Court has made Bruce Lehrmann's defamation case a complete failure

children.content: The High Court was Bruce Lehrmann's last legal avenue to challenge a Federal Court ruling that, on the balance of probabilities, he raped Brittany Higgins — now that avenue has closed.

gallery.altText: Bruce Lehrman leaves the Federal Court of Australia in Sydney.

thumbnail.altText: Bruce Lehrman leaves the Federal Court of Australia in Sydney.

items.title: Nitrous oxide a 'significant factor' in Canberra teen's drowning death

children.content: An 18-year-old was heavily impaired by nitrous oxide, known as "nangs", when his car entered a pond, prompting a coroner to call for a review of the gas's availability in the ACT.

image.altText: A pond with two road bridges going over it.

items.title: Teen dies after crashing bike at Canberra's Stromlo Forest Park

children.content: Police say the 14-year-old boy came off his bike while riding in Stromlo Forest Park on Saturday, and later died in hospital.

image.altText: A photo of the forest park with shrubbery, rocks and signs and blue sky in the distance.

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith display at War Memorial altered following arrest

children.content: As the Australian War Memorial unveils some of its newest elements as part of a $500 million redevelopment, director Matt Anderson confirms it has altered a display dedicated to Ben Roberts-Smith in the wake of his arrest for alleged war crimes.

gallery.altText: A man with short grey hair wearing a navy suit stands outdoors looking serious.

thumbnail.altText: A man with short grey hair wearing a navy suit stands outdoors looking serious.

avatar.alt: Niki Burnside is a journalist and digital producer based in Canberra.

items.title: Man jailed for role in major steroid and prescription drug syndicate

children.content: A Canberra man is jailed for his part in an illegal drug ring which operated as an online pharmacy, distributing steroids and prescription drugs across the country.

image.altText: A box of pills and drugs.

items.title: High Court refuses to hear Bruce Lehrmann's appeal

children.content: The High Court was Bruce Lehrmann's last legal avenue to challenge a Federal Court ruling that found, on the balance of probabilities, he did rape Brittany Higgins.

image.altText: Bruce walks outside, flanked by others, wearing a blue suit, white shirt and pink tie and glasses.

items.title: 'Sounds good to me': How Trump was sold war in Iran

children.content: Extraordinary reporting reveals how Israel made the case for regime change in Iran — and how the fallout is driving a profound shift in American opinion, amid deep uncertainty about what the war has achieved.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu shake hands.

images.altText: Paul Curtis attempts to palm off Ryan Lester.

children.content: North Melbourne faces its toughest challenge of the season so far against defending premiers Brisbane, while winless Essendon takes on the Demons. Follow live.

items.title: Bombers end record losing streak, Sydney lead Gold Coast

updates.title: We are underway at Adelaide Oval

items.title: Roosters on the verge of epic comeback

children.content: After a rollicking second half, the Roosters are edging closer to a mighty comeback win over the Sharks. Follow live.

image.altText: A man runs the ball during a rugby league match

items.title: Three dead after car hits tree in Perth Hills

children.content: Three people have died after a vehicle left the road and hit a tree in the Perth Hills suburb of Stoneville.

image.altText: A tree in Stoneville

items.title: Lying immobile after a remote chopper crash, Fred felt something biting his leg

children.content: In the aftermath of a 2019 helicopter crash in Kakadu National Park, Fred Hunter lay on the ground covered in blood and fuel, when he felt something biting his leg. On the 20th anniversary of CareFlight, Mr Hunter recounts his dramatic rescue by the service.

gallery.altText: Wide-shot, thigh-up of tan man, white / gray t-shirt, beige shorts, standing among tropical forest trees, looking up at sky

thumbnail.altText: Wide-shot, thigh-up of tan man, white / gray t-shirt, beige shorts, standing among tropical forest trees, looking up at sky

items.title: Hollingsworth sensationally reinstated as national champion after initial DQ

children.content: The twists and turns of the women's 1,500 metres final at the Australian Athletics Championships continue, with Claudia Hollingsworth winning her appeal against her disqualification after favourite Jess Hull crashed out.

image.altText: Claudia Hollingsworth has a distressed look on her face as she runs towards the finish

items.title: Three men arrested, weapons seized after police chase in Sydney's west

children.content: The occupants of the allegedly stolen Audi threw fuel jerry cans from the car onto the M4 Motorway while trying to evade police, officers say.

image.altText: nsw police officers on a highway standing next to a crashed car that was involved in a police chase

items.title: Debra Adelaide's best friend died by suicide. She wrote a novel about her

children.content: Debra Adelaide became friends with Gabrielle Carey when they were 12. After Carey died by suicide five decades later, Adelaide tried to address her unanswered questions in a novel.

gallery.altText: A woman with long grey hair and wearing a green long-sleeved top, sitting a garden and patting a dog.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with long grey hair and wearing a green long-sleeved top, sitting a garden and patting a dog.

items.title: This rich, meat-free curry is a budget-friendly way to make a massaman

children.content: Mushrooms are a cost-effective way to make a hearty massaman that is also vego and vegan-friendly.

image.altText: A plate of curry, containing mushrooms and potatoes, sits on rice on a plate. It is topped with chopped green coriander.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: A classic poem, oyster recipes and Itchy and Scratchy

children.content: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

image.altText: A cartoon cat and mouse sit on rocking chairs on a porch. Lemonade is on a table between them

items.title: Fans living it up in Adelaide for Gather Round but future of event uncertain

children.content: Occupancy rates and food and drink sales are up on last year's Gather Round, says the Australian Hotels Association, despite concerns fuel prices would deter interstate visitors.

gallery.altText: People in different club attires walk on a footbridge with Gather Round signs. A lit up Adelaide Oval in the distance at night

thumbnail.altText: People in different club attires walk on a footbridge with Gather Round signs. A lit up Adelaide Oval in the distance at night

items.title: War hero's looming prosecution divides opinion

children.content: Once celebrated as Australia's most decorated living war hero, the looming prosecution of Ben Roberts-Smith is shaping up to be the next trial of the century.

image.altText: a man leaning against a wall which holds a portrait of him wearing an army uniform

children.content: The High Court was Bruce Lehrmann's last legal avenue to challenge a Federal Court ruling that, on the balance of probabilities, he raped Brittany Higgins — now that avenue has closed.

items.title: Man dead as house destroyed in fire in Brisbane's south

children.content: Emergency services were called to a house fire on the corner of Foote and Annear streets in Acacia Ridge, where a man's body was found.

image.altText: Aerial view of a house destroyed by fire

items.title: Maila may be just a tropical low by the time it reaches Australia

children.content: After causing damage to islands in the Pacific, Cyclone Maila is downgraded as it heads towards Australia.

image.altText: A satellite image of cyclone Maila off the coast of far north Queensland.

items.title: Teen shuns screens to turn love of chickens into growing egg business

children.content: While many teenagers spend their spare time online gaming or playing sport, 14-year-old Max Lye hangs out with a bunch of chooks and dreams of owning his own egg farm.

gallery.altText: A teenage boy in a red shirt and cap holds two chickens.

items.title: Milk prices may need to increase to cover soaring fuel and fertiliser costs

children.content: Dairy farmers across Australia are battling crippling fuel and fertiliser costs. The industry is now calling for a 30-cent-per-litre rise in milk prices on supermarket shelves.

image.altText: A line of black and white cows lean their heads into a feeding trough. There is yellow hay in the trough.

items.title: McIlroy's 'just keep swinging' approach takes him to huge Masters lead

children.content: Jason Day does not do a lot wrong in the second round at Augusta National, but Rory McIlroy is looking just about untouchable after two rounds of the Masters.

image.altText: Fans watch Rory McIlroy tee off in the second round of the Masters.

items.title: Australians less likely to get bypass surgery now than 20 years ago

children.content: John Ross may be Australia's longest surviving double bypass patient. Here's how has the cardiac operation has evolved over the past 50 years.

image.altText: A black and white photo of a surgical operation.

items.title: UK man jailed for abusing girl may face renewed visa cancellation

children.content: A British child sex offender who avoided deportation is facing a possible visa review, after an ABC investigation.

image.altText: A swing at an Adelaide playground site.

items.title: 'They're not just big fluffy puppy dogs': Breeder warning over Highland cattle popularity

children.content: Registered Highland cattle breeders have more than tripled in Western Australia in the past five years. The shaggy Scottish breed is gaining popularity across WA, with more breeders interested in taking on the cattle and strong demand from agritourism.

gallery.altText: A tan highland cow with its nose very close to the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A tan highland cow with its nose very close to the camera.

items.title: Shark danger from rotting whale forces ocean swim event to move

children.content: A 40-tonne sperm whale carcass has forced the relocation of Tasmania's biggest open water swim. Organisers of the event had hoped it would be moved, but the state's environment department says that was not feasible.

image.altText: a pink and grey whale carcass in white and aqua waves

items.title: Country church with lone parishioner to close as modern worship evolves

children.content: Anglican priest David Perryman delivers Communion to a congregation of one in a small town about three hours east of Melbourne — but not for much longer.

image.altText: David Perryman

items.title: Fresh push for Murray Basin freight rail to get more produce off trucks

children.content: North-west Victorian farmers are pushing for the completion of the Murray Basin Rail Project, more than seven years after it was originally slated to be finished.

image.altText: old broad gauge rail track near Manangatang in Victoria's west

items.title: Greyhound trainers frustrated as safety issues continue at Queensland track

children.content: Ipswich facility The Q was promised to be "as safe for dogs and trainers as possible", but several dogs died during its trial period and events have been cancelled due to unsafe conditions.

image.altText: a greyhound racing track at sunset

items.title: With most of it not fit to sell, this op shop fixes donated items to reduce landfill

children.content: Every year, lots of new textile items are imported to the Australian market … about 1.55 billion of them. Fast fashion is a leading contributor to overproduction and makes reuse difficult.

image.altText: A woman looking at clothes on a rack in an op shop

items.title: Cost of leaving outback pushes students to train as teachers locally

children.content: Ashlee Toms and Aria Miller graduated from high school and walked back through the school gates as staff less than six months later.

image.altText: Two young teachers stand beside a wall with a sign reading 'Mount Isa Central State School'.
